issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible rule removes the default outline with outline: none and provides only outline-offset, resulting in no visible focus indicator for button elements when navigated via keyboard.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on #site-search button[type="submit"]
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button has an explicit width and height of 28px, which falls within the 24–43px range but has zero padding, failing to meet the 44px recommended touch target size and providing less than 8px of padding to compensate.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class sets a min-height of 32px (24–43px range) with vertical padding of only 7.5px (space-2-5), which is below the 8px threshold needed to compensate for the smaller explicit size, risking difficult activation for users with motor impairments.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm modifier sets min-height to 36px (24–43px range) but provides only 6px of vertical padding (space-1-5), failing to meet the 8px compensation padding needed to avoid a WCAG 2.5.8 Target Size (AA) failure for the recommended 44px size.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .form-group input[type="checkbox"]
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input has an explicit width and height of 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size required by WCAG 2.5.8 Target Size (AA), making it difficult for users with motor impairments to activate.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a
issuesFound.explanation: The footer navigation links have a min-height of 24px with no compensating padding, placing them at the absolute minimum size without meeting the recommended 44px touch target or providing 8px of padding to compensate.

issuesFound.title: Broken visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static and clip: auto, which makes the content visible in the layout and fails to hide it visually while keeping it accessible to assistive technologies. It should use position: absolute and clipping.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition property, causing spatial movement animations that are not disabled for users who prefer reduced motion, violating WCAG 2.3.3 Animation from Interactions (AAA).

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link class animates the transform property to slide the link, but there is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion) rule to disable this animation for users sensitive to motion.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-ctas .btn-primary
issuesFound.explanation: The hero CTA button includes transform in its transition, creating a spatial animation effect that lacks a prefers-reduced-motion media query override to respect user motion preferences.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on [id$="-submenu"] li a
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu links have text-decoration: none and rely solely on colour (neutral-900) to distinguish them from surrounding text, with no font-weight increase or other non-colour visual differentiator at rest, failing WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color (A).

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on .footer-brand address a
issuesFound.explanation: The address links in the footer brand section use text-decoration: none and rely only on colour (neutral-400) against surrounding text (neutral-500), with no additional visual differentiator such as font-weight or border-bottom to identify them as links.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links remove underlines and rely solely on colour (neutral-400) to distinguish them from the surrounding footer text (neutral-300), violating WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color (A) by not providing a non-colour visual indicator.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on .footer-bottom > p a
issuesFound.explanation: The footer bottom paragraph links use text-decoration: none and rely only on colour (neutral-400) to distinguish them from the surrounding paragraph text (neutral-500), with no font-weight change or other non-colour differentiator.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on .social-links a
issuesFound.explanation: Social media links in the footer have text-decoration: none and rely solely on colour (neutral-400) against the footer background, with no underline, font-weight increase, or border to indicate they are interactive links.

issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible selector sets outline: none and only provides outline-offset, which does not create a visible focus ring. Keyboard users cannot see which element has focus. The outline-offset property alone does not render any visible indicator.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size with insufficient padding on .btn-sm

issuesFound.title: Touch target at minimum size with no compensating padding on footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition property, causing spatial movement animations that are not disabled for users who prefer reduced motion. This can trigger vestibular disorders.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on #site-search button[type="submit"]
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button has explicit width and height of 28px (below the 24px minimum requirement) and zero padding, creating a touch target that is too small for users with motor impairments to activate reliably.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .form-group input[type="checkbox"]

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, and clip: auto, which makes the content visible in the layout rather than hiding it visually while keeping it accessible to screen readers. This defeats the purpose of the utility.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn:active
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:active selector applies a translateY transform without a prefers-reduced-motion override, causing spatial movement that may affect users with vestibular disorders when buttons are activated.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible selector sets outline: none and only provides outline-offset: 3px without a visible replacement such as box-shadow or border. This removes the focus indicator for keyboard users, making it impossible to see which element has focus.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset: 3px, providing no visible focus indicator replacement. Keyboard users cannot see when buttons receive focus.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size for checkbox input
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input has explicit width: 20px and height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size required by WCAG 2.5.8. Users with motor impairments may have difficulty activating the control.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1352:
```
.form-group input[type="checkbox"] { width: 24px; height: 24px; min-height: 24px; border: 2px solid var(--colour-border-strong); border-radius: var(--radius-base); cursor: pointer; flex-shrink: 0; accent-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); }
```

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size for .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 36px with vertical padding of only 6px (var(--space-1-5)), resulting in a touch target of 36px which is below the recommended 44px for comfortable activation.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size for search submit button
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button has explicit width: 28px and height: 28px, which is below the recommended 44px touch target size and may be difficult for users with motor impairments to activate.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size for footer navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: The footer navigation links have min-height: 24px with no padding specified, resulting in a touch target at the minimum 24px but below the recommended 44px for comfortable activation.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1414:
```
footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a { font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--colour-neutral-400); text-decoration: none; transition: color var(--transition-fast); line-height: var(--line-height-normal); display: inline-block; min-height: 44px; }
```

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override for .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition property and applies transform: translateY(1px) on :active, but has no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable this motion for users with vestibular disorders.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override for .pricing-card--featured:hover
issuesFound.explanation: The .pricing-card--featured:hover selector applies transform: translateY(-4px) scale(1.01) but is not covered by the existing prefers-reduced-motion media query, which only targets .pricing-card:hover without the --featured modifier.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast for table "not included" cells
issuesFound.explanation: The table cells with aria-label="Not included" and aria-label="Not available" use color: var(--colour-neutral-300) (#cbd5e1) against a white or near-white background, resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 1.4:1, which fails WCAG AA requirements of 4.5:1 for normal text.

issuesFound.title: Feature card paragraph colour contrast insufficient
issuesFound.explanation: The .feature-card p uses var(--colour-text-secondary) (#aaaaaa) on a white background, resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 2.3:1, which fails the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text.

issuesFound.title: Global focus-visible outline removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible pseudo-class sets outline: none and only adjusts outline-offset, which renders no visible focus indicator for keyboard users. This violates WCAG 2.4.11 Focus Appearance. Users navigating with keyboards cannot see which element has focus.

issuesFound.title: Small button touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size required by WCAG 2.5.8. Users with motor impairments may have difficulty activating this button.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces space between words and can make text difficult to read for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities.
issuesFound.fix: Line 163:   word-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: Button focus-visible outline removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector removes the default outline and only sets outline-offset, providing no visible focus ring. Keyboard users cannot see when buttons are focused.

issuesFound.title: Screen-reader-only utility class broken
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static and visible overflow, which renders the content visually and disrupts layout instead of hiding it visually while keeping it accessible to screen readers.

issuesFound.title: Search submit button touch target below recommended size
issuesFound.explanation: The #site-search button[type="submit"] has width: 28px and height: 28px with zero padding, falling below the 44px recommended size and potentially below the 24px minimum effective area. This makes the button difficult to activate.

issuesFound.title: Icon button touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px, below the 24px minimum. Icon-only buttons are difficult for users with motor impairments to activate.

issuesFound.title: Checkbox touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: The .form-group input[type="checkbox"] has width: 20px and height: 20px, below the 24px minimum touch target size. This makes checkboxes difficult for users with motor impairments to toggle.

issuesFound.title: Global link underline removed without alternative
issuesFound.explanation: The global a selector sets text-decoration: none without ensuring all links have a non-colour visual differentiator. While some components add underlines on hover, many navigation and footer links rely solely on colour, failing WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color.

issuesFound.title: Navigation toggle focus-visible outline removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .nav-toggle:focus-visible selector removes the outline without a visible replacement. This mobile menu button lacks a focus indicator for keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Submenu link focus-visible outline removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible selector removes the outline and only sets a negative outline-offset, providing no visible focus indicator for dropdown menu items.

issuesFound.title: Search submit button focus-visible outline removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible selector removes the outline without providing a visible alternative. The search button lacks a focus indicator.

issuesFound.title: Header logo link focus-visible outline removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .header-inner > a:first-child:focus-visible selector removes the outline without providing a visible alternative. Keyboard users cannot see focus on the logo link.

issuesFound.title: Account navigation link focus-visible outline removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible selector removes the outline without a visible replacement. Account links lack keyboard focus visibility.

issuesFound.title: Feature card link focus-visible outline removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .feature-card a:focus-visible selector removes the outline without providing a visible alternative. The "Learn more" links lack focus indicators.

issuesFound.title: FAQ button focus-visible outline removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .faq-item dt button:focus-visible selector removes the outline and sets a negative offset without a visible replacement. FAQ accordion buttons lack focus visibility.

issuesFound.title: Form input focus-visible outline removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .form-group input:focus-visible, .form-group select:focus-visible, .form-group textarea:focus-visible selectors remove the outline without providing a visible alternative. Form fields lack focus indicators.

issuesFound.title: Checkbox focus-visible outline removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .form-group input[type="checkbox"]:focus-visible selector removes the outline without a visible replacement. Checkboxes lack keyboard focus indicators.

issuesFound.title: Social link focus-visible outline removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .social-links a:focus-visible selector removes the outline without a visible replacement. Social media links lack keyboard focus indicators.

issuesFound.title: Footer navigation link focus-visible outline removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible selector removes the outline without providing a visible alternative. Footer links lack focus visibility.

issuesFound.title: Table wrapper focus-visible outline removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .table-wrapper:focus-visible selector removes the outline without providing a visible alternative. The scrollable table region lacks a focus indicator when focused via keyboard.

issuesFound.title: Button transform transition missing reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition property and has an :active state with translateY(1px), but no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override exists to disable this spatial movement for users with vestibular disorders.

issuesFound.title: Hero image transform transition missing reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img has a transition on transform that creates spatial movement on hover, but the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block for this selector is empty, failing to disable the animation.

issuesFound.title: Hero CTA button transform transition missing reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-ctas .btn-primary includes transform in its transition and applies translateY(-1px) on hover without a prefers-reduced-motion override to disable the spatial movement.

issuesFound.title: Featured pricing card transform missing reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .pricing-card--featured:hover applies translateY(-4px) and scale(1.01) transforms, but the existing @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) only covers .pricing-card:hover, not the featured variant.

issuesFound.title: Step content paragraph colour contrast insufficient
issuesFound.explanation: The .step-content p uses var(--colour-text-secondary) (#aaaaaa) on a white background, resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 2.3:1, failing WCAG 1.4.3 Contrast (Minimum).

issuesFound.title: Step content code colour contrast insufficient

errorMessage: Ollama HTTP 500: {"StatusCode":500,"Status":"500 Internal Server Error","error":"Internal Server Error (ref: 4753c710-9c0f-49de-bbf8-8fe635ec02c3)"}

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() omits aria-expanded update on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open() function adds CSS classes to display the navigation drawer and body scroll lock, but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. This leaves screen reader users unaware that the menu has expanded, even though the close() function correctly sets aria-expanded="false".
issuesFound.fix:
Line: Inside mobileNav.open(), immediately after the line `_open = true;`:
    setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() omits aria-expanded update on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When opening a dropdown panel, the function removes the hidden attribute and adds CSS classes but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger element. This prevents assistive technologies from announcing the expanded state to users.
issuesFound.fix:
Line: Inside dropdownMenu._openInstance(), immediately after the line `inst.panel.removeAttribute('hidden');`:
    setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() omits aria-expanded update on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The function opens an FAQ item by setting item.open = true and adding CSS classes, but does not set the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button to "true". This is only set to "false" in the close() function, creating an asymmetry that hides the expanded state from assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix:
Line: Inside faqAccordion.open(), immediately after the line `addClass(item.el, CSS.open);`:
    setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid() fails to set aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: When marking a form field as invalid, the function adds the error element to aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field itself. This prevents screen readers from identifying the field as having an error, which is required for WCAG 2.2 form validation accessibility.
issuesFound.fix:
Line: Inside srUtils.markInvalid(), immediately after the line `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`:
    setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');

issuesFound.title: productFilter tabs lack aria-selected state indication
issuesFound.explanation: The function updates the active CSS class on filter tab buttons based on the current category, but does not set aria-selected to indicate which tab is currently active. This makes it impossible for screen reader users to determine which filter is currently applied.
issuesFound.fix:
Line: Inside productFilter._syncTabAriaStates(), within the forEach loop after `const isActive = ...`:
    setAttr(btn, 'aria-selected', String(isActive));

issuesFound.title: productFilter filter tabs may be non-button elements without keyboard support
issuesFound.explanation: The code attaches click listeners to elements matching '[data-filter-value]' but does not ensure they are native button elements or programmatically add role="button", tabindex="0", and Enter/Space key handlers. If the HTML uses divs or spans for these tabs, they will be inaccessible to keyboard users who cannot click.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open function does not update aria-expanded to "true"
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open function adds classes and announces the state change but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The close function correctly sets it to "false", creating a state mismatch where screen readers are not informed the menu is open.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance function does not update aria-expanded to "true"
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance function removes the hidden attribute and adds classes but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger. The corresponding _closeInstance function sets it to "false", leaving the expanded state incorrect for assistive technology.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance function does not announce state change to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: Opening a dropdown menu changes visible content but does not announce this change via a live region. Screen reader users are not notified that a menu has opened, unlike the mobileNav module which does announce.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeInstance function does not announce state change to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: Closing a dropdown menu changes visible content but does not announce this change via a live region. Screen reader users are not notified that a menu has closed.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open function does not update aria-expanded to "true"
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open function sets the item state and animates the panel but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The close function correctly sets it to "false", causing a discrepancy in the accessibility tree.

issuesFound.title: cookieConsent._showBanner function does not announce banner appearance
issuesFound.explanation: When the cookie consent banner is dynamically injected and shown, no announcement is made to screen readers. Users may be unaware a modal dialog has appeared requiring interaction.

fpTitles: productFilter filter tabs may be non-button elements without keyboard support
dropdownMenu._openInstance function does not update aria-expanded to "true"
faqAccordion.open function does not update aria-expanded to "true"

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid() omits aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The function links error messages to fields via `aria-describedby` but fails to set `aria-invalid="true"`. Without this attribute, screen readers cannot identify which fields contain invalid data.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1385: Add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` as the first line inside the `markInvalid` function.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() omits aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer opens, the function updates CSS classes and body state but does not set `aria-expanded="true"` on the trigger button. Screen readers will not announce the expanded state to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2153: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after the `_open = true;` statement.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() omits aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The function reveals the dropdown panel by removing the hidden attribute but fails to set `aria-expanded="true"` on the trigger element. The companion `_closeInstance` correctly sets it to "false", creating an inconsistent state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2455: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `inst.open = true;`.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() omits aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: When expanding an FAQ item, the function animates the panel and updates state but does not set `aria-expanded="true"` on the question button trigger. Screen readers cannot convey the expanded state to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4125: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `item.open = true;`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() missing focus management
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer opens, focus remains on the trigger button instead of moving to the drawer or its first focusable element. Keyboard users must tab through the entire page to reach the newly revealed content.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2165: Add `const firstFocusable = getFocusableChildren(_drawer)[0]; if (firstFocusable) firstFocusable.focus();` immediately after `_lockScroll();`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close() missing focus return
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation closes (via Escape or outside click), focus is not returned to the trigger button that opened it. This causes keyboard users to lose their place in the page tab order.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2205: Add `_trigger.focus();` immediately before the `emit(_trigger, 'mobilenav:close', { drawer: _drawer });` line.

issuesFound.title: Form validation utility fails to set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markInvalid function adds an aria-describedby reference but never sets aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Screen readers will not announce the field as invalid, only that it has a description.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation open function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open function adds the open class and announces the state but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen readers will not know the menu is expanded.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation close function lacks live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.close function sets aria-expanded="false" but does not announce the state change to screen readers via the live region, unlike the open function. Users may not know the menu closed.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open instance does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance function reveals the panel but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger. The corresponding close function sets it to false, creating a state mismatch.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu state changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance and _closeInstance functions emit custom events but do not update an aria-live region. Users relying on screen readers are not notified when dropdowns open or close.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open function expands the panel and announces the action but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The close function correctly sets it to false.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion close function lacks live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.close function collapses the panel and updates aria-expanded but does not announce the change to the live region. The open function does announce, creating an inconsistent experience.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() missing aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown panel is opened, the function removes the hidden attribute and adds CSS classes, but fails to update the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute to "true", leaving assistive technologies unaware of the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2580: Add setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); immediately after inst.panel.removeAttribute('hidden');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() missing focus trap activation
issuesFound.explanation: The function creates a focus trap instance during initialization but never activates it when opening the mobile navigation drawer. Keyboard focus is not confined to the drawer while it is open, violating WCAG 2.4.3 Focus Order.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2440: Add _trap.activate(); immediately after _lockScroll(); in the open() function;

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu open/close missing live region announcements
issuesFound.explanation: Opening and closing dropdown menus changes the visible UI state but does not announce these changes to screen reader users via the liveRegion announcer, leaving users unaware that content has appeared or disappeared.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2580 (in _openInstance): Add liveRegion.announce('Dropdown menu opened'); Line 2590 (in _closeInstance): Add liveRegion.announce('Dropdown menu closed');

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() missing aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The function expands the FAQ answer panel using animateHeight and removes the hidden attribute, but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button to communicate the expanded state to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4220: Add setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); immediately after the line item.open = true;

issuesFound.title: activeFilterChips.render() missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The function dynamically adds or removes filter chip elements from the DOM when filters change, but does not announce these changes to screen readers, so users are not informed that filters have been applied or removed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3400: Add liveRegion.announce(`Filter ${c.label} applied`); when adding chips and liveRegion.announce(`Filter removed`); when removing them;

issuesFound.title: multiStepWizard._goToStep() missing aria-live announcement for step changes
issuesFound.explanation: The function transitions between wizard steps and updates the progress bar, but the visual step change is not announced to screen readers beyond the focus move. Users may not realize they have advanced to a new step.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4750: Ensure the liveRegion.announce call includes the step title and number: liveRegion.announce(`Step ${_current + 1} of ${_steps.length}: ${entering.title}`); (Note: the function already has this announcement, verify it is present and working);

issuesFound.title: productFilter._syncTabAriaStates() missing aria-selected
issuesFound.explanation: Filter tab buttons indicate the active category using CSS classes only. They lack the aria-selected attribute required for tab interfaces to convey selection state to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3330: Add setAttr(btn, 'aria-selected', String(isActive)); inside the forEach loop after toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid() missing aria-invalid="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The function marks a form field as invalid by linking an error message via aria-describedby, but it fails to set the aria-invalid attribute to "true". Screen readers may not convey the invalid state to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1720: Add field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true'); inside the markInvalid function before or after the addDescribedBy call;

issuesFound.title: fileUploadField._uploadEntry() missing aria-live progress announcements
issuesFound.explanation: During file upload, the progress bar value is updated but the percentage changes are not announced to screen readers, leaving users unaware of upload progress until completion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4500: Add liveRegion.announce(`${entry.progress} percent uploaded`); inside the progress event handler when entry.progress reaches 100 or at 25/50/75 milestones;

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle function does not update aria-expanded on open
issuesFound.explanation: The `_openInstance` function in the `dropdownMenu` module updates the visual state of the dropdown (removes `hidden`, adds class) but fails to set `aria-expanded="true"` on the trigger element. While `_closeInstance` correctly sets `aria-expanded="false"` (line 1863), the open state is never explicitly announced to assistive technology via this attribute, causing a mismatch between visual and programmatic state.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu open action not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `_openInstance` function in `dropdownMenu` changes visible content (opens the panel) but does not write to an `aria-live` region. Unlike `mobileNav` (line 1683) and `faqAccordion` (line 3017), this module does not announce the state change to screen readers, which is required for dynamic status messages (WCAG 4.1.3).

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu close action not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `_closeInstance` function in `dropdownMenu` hides the panel but does not announce this change to an `aria-live` region. Screen reader users are not informed when the menu collapses, degrading the experience for those relying on audio feedback for state changes.

issuesFound.title: cookieConsent banner appearance not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `_showBanner` function in `cookieConsent` dynamically injects and displays a dialog but does not announce its appearance to an `aria-live` region. While focus is trapped and moved to the banner, an initial announcement ensures users are aware of the interruption immediately.

issuesFound.title: activeNavLinks state change not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `init` function in `activeNavLinks` updates `aria-current="page"` on navigation links to reflect the current URL but does not announce this change to an `aria-live` region. Users are not informed which page is active via audio feedback.

issuesFound.title: scrollSpy activation not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `_activate` function in `scrollSpy` updates `aria-current` on navigation links as the user scrolls but does not announce the section change to an `aria-live` region. This prevents screen reader users from knowing which section of the page is currently in view.

issuesFound.title: pricing highlightPlan action not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `highlightPlan` function in `pricing` updates the visual and `aria-label` state of a plan card to indicate recommendation but does not announce this change to an `aria-live` region. If called dynamically, screen reader users are not informed which plan is highlighted.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open function visually displays the navigation drawer by adding CSS classes and removing the hidden attribute from the overlay, but it does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed that the menu is now expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1108: After `addClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open);`, add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.close function hides the navigation drawer but does not set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button to indicate the collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1128: After `removeClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open);`, add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.init missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation module initializes, it does not set the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button to reflect the initial closed state of the drawer.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1148: After `_initialised = true;`, add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance function in dropdownMenu reveals the dropdown panel but fails to update aria-expanded to "true" on the trigger element, leaving screen readers unaware the menu is open.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1154: After `inst.open = true;`, add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeInstance missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The _closeInstance function hides the dropdown panel but does not set aria-expanded to "false" on the trigger element.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1165: After `inst.open = false;`, add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: When opening an FAQ item, the function sets item.open to true and reveals the panel, but it does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The close function correctly sets it to false, creating an inconsistent state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1559: After `item.open = true;`, add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch input missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The suggestionListRenderer.mount function assigns role="combobox" and aria-controls to the search input, but it does not initialize aria-expanded to "false" when the suggestion listbox is hidden on page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1313: After `setAttr(input, 'aria-haspopup', 'listbox');`, add `setAttr(input, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid utility function adds an error message reference via aria-describedby but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the field. Screen readers will not announce the invalid state to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1018: After `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`, add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The markValid utility function removes the error description reference but does not remove the aria-invalid attribute or set it to "false", potentially leaving stale invalid states on fields that have become valid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1029: At the end of the function, add `removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid');`

issuesFound.title: pricing.setPeriod missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period is toggled between monthly and annual, the prices update visually across all plan cards, but there is no announcement via an aria-live region to inform screen reader users of the price change.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1414: After `emit(document, 'pricing:periodchange', { from: prev, to: period });`, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.pricingPeriodChanged || 'Prices updated to ' + period + ' billing');`

issuesFound.title: productFilter.applyFilters missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied to the product grid, the visible card count changes and cards are shown or hidden, but no announcement is made to screen readers to indicate the results count or that the filter has been applied.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1494: After `emit(_gridEl, 'filter:applied', { count: totalMatches, state: Object.assign({}, s) });`, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.filterApplied || 'Showing ' + totalMatches + ' products');`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open/close missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When FAQ items are expanded or collapsed, the state change is not announced to screen readers, leaving users unaware that content has appeared or disappeared.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1559: Add `liveRegion.announce(item.question + ' expanded');` after `addClass(item.el, CSS.open);`. Line 1576: Add `liveRegion.announce(item.question + ' collapsed');` after `removeClass(item.el, CSS.open);`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu open/close missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When dropdown menus open or close, the state change is not announced to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1154: Add `liveRegion.announce('Menu opened');` after `inst.panel.removeAttribute('hidden');`. Line 1165: Add `liveRegion.announce('Menu closed');` after `inst.panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close missing focus return to trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer is closed, focus is not returned to the trigger button that opened it. This violates WCAG 2.4.3 Focus Order, leaving keyboard users with lost focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1128: At the end of the close function (before the emit call), add `if (_trigger) _trigger.focus();`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid omits aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: When marking a form field as invalid, the function adds aria-describedby to link the error message but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field itself. Screen reader users cannot identify that the field contains an error.
issuesFound.fix: Line: In markInvalid(), after addDescribedBy(field, errorEl); add: setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation module initializes, the trigger button does not receive an initial aria-expanded="false" attribute. Screen reader users are not informed that the button controls an expandable region that is initially collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line: In mobileNav.init(), after _trigger = qs(SELECTORS.navToggle); add: if (_trigger) setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: pricing module missing live region for period change
issuesFound.explanation: When users switch between monthly and annual billing periods, the price values update visually with animation, but the change is not announced to screen reader users, leaving them unaware of the new pricing information.
issuesFound.fix: Line: In setPeriod(), after planDataParser.getAll().forEach(plan => _updatePlanCard(plan, period)); add: liveRegion.announce(`Prices updated to ${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label} billing`);

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu panel toggle omits aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu functions show and hide the panel by manipulating the hidden attribute and CSS classes, but they fail to synchronise the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button. Screen reader users are not informed when the dropdown opens or closes.
issuesFound.fix: Line: In _openInstance, after inst.panel.removeAttribute('hidden'); add: setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); Line: In _closeInstance, after the setTimeout block (or before returnFocus), add: setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: productFilter tabs omit aria-pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The category filter tab buttons toggle the active filter visually via CSS classes but do not update aria-pressed to communicate the active state to screen reader users. This prevents users from knowing which filter is currently applied.
issuesFound.fix: Line: In _syncTabAriaStates(), inside the forEach loop after const isActive = ...; add: setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid omits aria-invalid clearance
issuesFound.explanation: When clearing validation state from a field, the function removes aria-describedby but does not clear the aria-invalid attribute, potentially leaving the field marked as invalid after it has been corrected.
issuesFound.fix: Line: In markValid(), after the if/else block handling aria-describedby, add: field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer lacks focus management on open
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer opens, focus remains on the trigger button instead of moving into the drawer or to the first focusable element within it. Keyboard users cannot perceive the newly opened content without manually navigating into it.
issuesFound.fix: Line: In mobileNav.open(), after addClass(document.body, CSS.navOpen); add: const firstFocusable = getFocusableChildren(_drawer)[0] || _drawer; if (firstFocusable) firstFocusable.focus();

issuesFound.title: pricing period buttons use incorrect aria-selected
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons use aria-selected to indicate the active state, but this attribute is not appropriate for standard button elements. They should use aria-pressed for toggle buttons.
issuesFound.fix: Line: In _syncToggleState(), change setAttr(btn, 'aria-selected', String(isActive)); to setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator _renderResult missing explicit aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: While _renderResult calls srUtils.markInvalid/markValid, it does not explicitly set aria-invalid on the field as a defensive measure. Given that srUtils currently omits this attribute, validation states are not communicated to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line: In _renderResult(), at the start of the function add: setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', String(!result.valid));

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle functions do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `open()` and `close()` functions in the `mobileNav` module change the visibility of the navigation drawer and emit events, but they do not update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the `_trigger` button. Screen reader users will not be informed of the menu's open/closed state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1653: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');
  Line 1683: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger aria-expanded not initialised at page load
issuesFound.explanation: The `init()` function in `mobileNav` queries the `_trigger` element and attaches event listeners, but it does not set the initial `aria-expanded` attribute (e.g., to "false") if it is missing from the HTML. This leaves the initial state ambiguous to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1706: if (!_trigger.hasAttribute('aria-expanded')) setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `_openInstance()` and `_closeInstance()` functions in the `dropdownMenu` module manage the visibility of dropdown panels and update internal state, but they fail to set `aria-expanded` on the `inst.trigger` element. This breaks the disclosure pattern for screen readers.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1883: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');
  Line 1898: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not set aria-expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The `open()` function in `faqAccordion` sets `item.open = true` and updates classes, but it does not set `aria-expanded="true"` on the `item.trigger`. The `close()` function correctly sets it to "false", creating an inconsistent state where the attribute is never updated to "true" via JS.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4156: setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');

issuesFound.title: srUtils markInvalid does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `markInvalid()` utility function adds an error description via `aria-describedby` but fails to set `aria-invalid="true"` on the invalid field. This prevents screen readers from announcing that the field is in an error state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1348: field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');

issuesFound.title: srUtils markValid does not clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `markValid()` utility function removes the error description but does not remove the `aria-invalid` attribute or set it to "false". The field may remain stuck in an invalid state in the accessibility tree even after correction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1358: field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');

issuesFound.title: productFilter count update not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `_updateCountBadge()` function in `productFilter` updates the visible product count text and `aria-label`, but it does not announce this change to the `liveRegion`. Screen reader users filtering products will not know how many results match their selection.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3203: liveRegion.announce(`${count} product${count !== 1 ? 's' : ''} found`);

issuesFound.title: pricing period change not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `setPeriod()` function in `pricing` updates price displays and emits a custom event, but it does not announce the billing period change (e.g., "Prices switched to annual billing") to the `liveRegion`. Users are not informed of the context change.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3639: liveRegion.announce(period === 'month' ? STRINGS.announce.pricingMonthly : STRINGS.announce.pricingAnnual);

issuesFound.title: themeSwitcher toggle elements missing keyboard support
issuesFound.explanation: The `themeSwitcher` module attaches a `click` listener to `[data-theme-toggle]` elements and manages `aria-pressed`, implying they act as buttons. However, no `keydown` listener is attached to handle Enter or Space keys. If these elements are not native `<button>` tags, they will be inaccessible to keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 6033: document.addEventListener('keydown', function _onThemeKeydown(ev) { const btn = closest(ev.target, '[data-theme-toggle]'); if (!btn) return; if (ev.key === KEYS.ENTER || ev.key === KEYS.SPACE) { ev.preventDefault(); btn.click(); } });

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing aria-expanded initialization

issuesFound.fix: Line 1418: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after `_initialised = true;` in the init function.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button does not have an aria-controls attribute pointing to the drawer element's id, failing to establish the programmatic relationship required by the ARIA disclosure pattern.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1419: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', _drawer.id || srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'mobile-nav'));` in the init function.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open does not move focus to drawer
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation opens, keyboard focus remains on the trigger button instead of moving to the drawer container or the first focusable element within it. Keyboard users cannot immediately navigate the opened menu.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1342: Add `requestAnimationFrame(() => { const first = getFocusableChildren(_drawer)[0] || _drawer; first.focus({ preventScroll: true }); });` at the end of the open function.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav close does not return focus to trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation closes, focus is not returned to the trigger button that opened it. Keyboard users lose their place in the focus order.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1362: Add `_trigger.focus();` before or after the call to `_unlockScroll();` in the close function.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav close missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer closes, no announcement is made to screen readers, leaving users unaware that the menu has disappeared.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1363: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');` in the close function.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing focus trap
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation is open, keyboard focus is not trapped within the drawer. Users can tab out of the menu into the background content, which should be inert.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1343: Add `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: _trigger }); _trap.activate();` in the open function. Line 1364: Add `if (_trap) { _trap.deactivate(); _trap = null; }` in the close function.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu open missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown menu opens, the change in context is not announced to screen reader users, who may not realize that a menu has appeared.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1668: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened || 'Menu opened');` inside _openInstance after the panel is shown.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu close missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown menu closes (especially via Escape key or focus leaving), no announcement is made to confirm that the menu has closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1688: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed || 'Menu closed');` inside _closeInstance.

issuesFound.title: pricing setPeriod missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When users switch between monthly and annual billing periods, the price changes visually but are not announced to screen reader users, leaving them unaware of the updated values.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2782: Add `liveRegion.announce((STRINGS.pricingPeriodChanged || 'Prices updated to {period} billing').replace('{period}', BILLING_PERIODS[period].label));` after updating the plan cards.

issuesFound.title: productFilter applyFilters missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied to the product grid, the results change visually but no announcement is made to screen readers regarding the number of results or that filtering has occurred.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2295: Add `liveRegion.announce((STRINGS.filterApplied || 'Showing {n} product{s}').replace('{n}', String(totalMatches)).replace('{s}', totalMatches !== 1 ? 's' : ''));` after the filter animation completes.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid not clearing aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When a form field transitions from invalid to valid, the markValid function removes the aria-describedby reference but does not remove or set aria-invalid to "false", leaving the field incorrectly marked as invalid for screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2895: Add `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');` or `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false');` inside the markValid function after removing the describedby reference.

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator not setting aria-invalid on validation
issuesFound.explanation: When form fields are validated, the code adds error styling and messages but never sets the aria-invalid attribute on the input field. Screen readers cannot convey to users which fields contain invalid data.
issuesFound.fix:
Line (in _renderResult, inside `if (result.valid) {` block, before closing brace): `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, false);`
  Line (in _renderResult, inside `else {` block, after `addClass(field, CSS.fieldInvalid);`): `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, true);`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown panel visibility is toggled by removing/adding the hidden attribute and CSS classes, but the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute is never updated. Assistive technologies cannot announce the expanded/collapsed state of the dropdown menus.
issuesFound.fix:
Line (in _openInstance, after `inst.open = true;`): `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);`
  Line (in _closeInstance, after `inst.open = false;`): `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid not setting aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The utility function marks a field as invalid by linking an error message via aria-describedby, but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field itself. This prevents screen readers from identifying the field as invalid in their "invalid entry" announcements.
issuesFound.fix: Line (at end of markInvalid function): `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, true);`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The search input has role="combobox" and controls a suggestion listbox, but the aria-expanded attribute is never updated when the suggestion panel opens or closes. Screen readers cannot convey whether the autocomplete suggestions are currently visible.
issuesFound.fix:
Line (in _open, after `_searchState.open = true;`): `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);`
  Line (in _close, after `_searchState.open = false;`): `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button is initialized without an aria-expanded attribute. Since the drawer is closed by default, the trigger must explicitly declare aria-expanded="false" so screen readers announce the initial collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line (in init, after `_initialised = true;` and before event listeners): `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`

issuesFound.title: pricing billing period buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period selection buttons (monthly/quarterly/annual) toggle the active state visually via CSS classes but do not update aria-pressed. Since these buttons act as toggle buttons (selecting one deselects others), they must communicate their pressed state to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line (inside _periodBtns.forEach, after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`): `setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, isActive);`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When registering a dropdown, the trigger button is not initialized with aria-expanded="false". Since the panel is hidden by default, the trigger must declare its initial collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line (in register, after `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');`): `setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The search input with role="combobox" is initialized without aria-expanded="false". Since the suggestion panel is closed by default, the input must declare its initial collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line (in init, after `const listbox = suggestionListRenderer.mount(_inputEl);`): `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The trigger button does not have an aria-controls attribute referencing the drawer element. Screen reader users cannot identify which element is controlled by the toggle button.

issuesFound.fix: Line: <IconButton aria-label="Close navigation menu" onClick={onClose} className="mobile-nav__close">

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel previous button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The carousel previous button contains only the ‹ character and lacks an aria-label. Screen reader users cannot identify the button's purpose or direction.
issuesFound.fix: Line: <IconButton aria-label="Previous testimonial" aria-controls={trackId} onClick={handlePrev} className="testimonials-track__prev">

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel next button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The carousel next button contains only the › character and lacks an aria-label. Screen reader users cannot identify the button's purpose or direction.
issuesFound.fix: Line: <IconButton aria-label="Next testimonial" aria-controls={trackId} onClick={handleNext} className="testimonials-track__next">

issuesFound.title: Textarea component missing aria-invalid mapping
issuesFound.explanation: The Textarea component accepts an invalid prop for styling but does not expose it as aria-invalid to assistive technology. Screen reader users are not notified when the field is in an error state.
issuesFound.fix: Line: <textarea id={id} className={['form-textarea', invalid ? 'form-textarea--error' : '', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} aria-invalid={invalid || undefined} {...rest} />

issuesFound.title: Select component missing aria-invalid mapping
issuesFound.explanation: The Select component accepts an invalid prop for visual error indication but does not set aria-invalid. Screen reader users cannot detect when an invalid option is selected or when validation fails.
issuesFound.fix: Line: <select id={id} className={['form-select', invalid ? 'form-select--error' : '', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} aria-invalid={invalid || undefined} {...rest}>

issuesFound.title: SkipLinks navigation landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The page contains multiple navigation landmarks (SkipLinks, DesktopNav, MobileNav, Footer nav). The SkipLinks nav lacks an aria-label, making it impossible for screen reader users to distinguish it from other navigation regions when using landmark navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line: <nav aria-label="Skip links">

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav navigation landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The desktop navigation landmark lacks an aria-label. With multiple nav elements on the page, screen reader users cannot distinguish the main navigation from footer or skip navigation regions.
issuesFound.fix: Line: <nav id="main-nav" aria-label="Main" className="desktop-nav">

issuesFound.title: StatsBar section missing aria-labelledby
issuesFound.explanation: The statistics section contains a heading with id="stats-heading" but the section element does not reference it via aria-labelledby. Screen reader users cannot identify the purpose of this landmark region when navigating by landmarks.
issuesFound.fix: Line: <section className="stats-bar" aria-labelledby="stats-heading">

issuesFound.title: HeroSection landmark missing aria-labelledby
issuesFound.explanation: The hero section contains a heading with id="hero-heading" but lacks aria-labelledby on the section element. Screen reader users cannot identify this landmark region when navigating by landmarks.
issuesFound.fix: Line: <section className="hero-section" aria-labelledby="hero-heading">

issuesFound.title: PlanCard article missing aria-labelledby
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing plan card is an article element containing a heading with id={headingId}, but it lacks aria-labelledby. Screen reader users cannot identify the specific plan when navigating by article regions or when the card receives focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line: <article className="pricing-card__article" aria-labelledby={headingId}>

issuesFound.title: FormGroup required marker not hidden from AT
issuesFound.explanation: The visual asterisk marker for required fields is rendered as text content without aria-hidden. Screen readers announce "star" or "asterisk" in addition to the visually hidden "(required)" text, creating redundant and potentially confusing announcements.
issuesFound.fix: Line: *

issuesFound.title: Button loading spinner not hidden from AT
issuesFound.explanation: The loading spinner visual indicator is not hidden from assistive technology. The button already includes a VisuallyHidden loading label, so the spinner is decorative and should be hidden to prevent redundant announcements.
issuesFound.fix: Line: <span className="btn-spinner" aria-hidden="true" />

issuesFound.title: Button component missing aria-busy during loading
issuesFound.explanation: When the button is in a loading state (isLoading=true), it does not declare aria-busy. Screen reader users are not notified that the button is processing an action and temporarily unavailable.
issuesFound.fix: Line: <button type={type} disabled={isDisabled} aria-disabled={isDisabled || undefined} aria-busy={isLoading} className={['btn', `btn--${variant}`, `btn--${size}`, className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest}>

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav current link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The current page link in the desktop navigation is visually indicated via CSS but lacks aria-current="page". Screen reader users cannot identify which page is currently active when navigating the menu.
issuesFound.fix: Line: {item.label}

issuesFound.title: IntegrationsBar cloned list missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The duplicated list used for the scrolling marquee animation is not hidden from assistive technology. Screen reader users encounter the same integration links twice, creating redundant noise and confusion.
issuesFound.fix: Line: <ul role="list" className="integrations-bar__list" aria-hidden="true">

issuesFound.title: StarRating inner stars missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The individual star symbols are decorative content within a composite widget that has an aria-label on its container. Without aria-hidden, screen readers may announce each star character individually (e.g., "black star black star black star") instead of the concise rating value provided by the container's aria-label.
issuesFound.fix: Line: {filled ? '★' : '☆'}

issuesFound.title: ContactForm message input has broken aria-describedby reference

'contact-message-error' : '', ].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} />
